methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Commonwealth Fusion Systems is a Series B2-stage frontier climate and energy company that has raised over $3 billion to date. The valuation universe consists of nine peers within Climate & Energy—one public company (Lnza) and eight private firms including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the peer set lacks sufficient density at comparable stage and valuation tiers to support traditional peer-median methodology; no three or more datapoints exist within the narrow filters of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier companies.

Standard triangulation approaches prove inapplicable given the data constraints. The single public comparable offers limited insight into an early-stage private company's valuation, and the private peer set is too dispersed across stages and business models to extract reliable median multiples. The absence of sufficient same-stage or tier-matched comparables reflects the specialized nature of frontier energy technology development.

Given these constraints, valuation analysis for Commonwealth Fusion Systems cannot be meaningfully triangulated from the available peer universe. Any defensible range would require either deeper proprietary data on private peer rounds and revenue multiples, or alternative methods such as discounted cash flow or venture capital method analysis grounded in specific technical and commercialization milestones. The primary uncertainty is the lack of comparable market transactions at equivalent stage and scale within the frontier climate sector.
